caption: Closes #996: a queued waypoint leg now applies its own recorded stance once the route promotes it, instead of silently inheriting whatever stance was already active. Both regiments start on Hold. The near (south) regiment's first leg marches under Hold; a second leg is then queued under Hold as well (arming a stance before a Shift+right-click waypoint append stamps that stance onto just the newly queued leg). Once the first leg finishes and the queue promotes the second, the regiment correctly holds at its new position instead of auto-advancing into the stationary far regiment 130 world units away -- before this fix, the appended leg's stance was captured but never applied, so the promoted leg would have silently kept whatever stance was active before it, and the regiment would have marched into a fight neither side was ordered to start.
